sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Bournemouth have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses, struggling to maintain consistency. Leicester City, on the other hand, have a slightly better recent form with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, showing some resilience despite their lower league position.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Leicester City won 1-0 at home, while the other game ended in a 0-0 draw (with Leicester scoring in extra time in a cup match). This suggests tight contests with low-scoring outcomes between these teams.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Bournemouth rank 11th with 56 goals scored and a strong attacking presence (high penalty area entries and final third possessions), while Leicester City, at 18th, have struggled defensively, conceding 78 goals. Bournemouth's superior expected goals and chance creation give them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Bournemouth's key attackers like Justin Kluivert (12 goals) and Evanilson (10 goals) are in the lineup, though they miss some midfield depth. Leicester City are without key forward Jamie Vardy (9 goals), which could impact their scoring threat.
